asset_type: TaraHome technical blog cover for a non-product Matter controller migration article
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome technical blog cover, landscape composition designed to survive both a 16:10 article crop and a centered 16:9 card crop
Primary request: create a clean editorial photograph about moving already-installed Matter smart-home devices from a voice-assistant ecosystem to a local Home Assistant hub without factory-resetting them
Scene/backdrop: a warm, lived-in residential dining nook with an oak table, off-white plaster wall, linen curtain, and soft natural morning light
Subject: a homeowner's hands calmly organizing a few ordinary, commercially plausible smart-home items on the table: one compact white smart plug, one small door/contact sensor, a low-profile local smart-home hub connected by a real Ethernet cable, a small mesh-covered smart speaker, and a phone lying face-down; arrange the local hub and devices as the clear practical focus, with the speaker slightly farther back to suggest a careful handoff without any symbolic graphics
Style/medium: high-end but natural residential editorial photography, realistic materials and proportions, subtle everyday texture, not a studio product lineup and not a conceptual diagram
Composition/framing: wide landscape, slight three-quarter overhead view, all essential objects and hands kept within the central 80 percent so both 16:10 and 16:9 crops work; balanced negative space; no object cut off
Lighting/mood: soft window light, calm and reassuring, warm wood and cream tones with one restrained dark-green accent in a small ceramic tray or textile
Materials/textures: natural oak grain, matte plastic, woven speaker fabric, linen, ceramic, realistic Ethernet cable
Constraints: no readable phone screen, no logos, no trademarks, no brand names, no invented futuristic hardware, no embedded headline or any text, no watermark
Avoid: headline poster, charts, graphs, diagrams, arrows, decorative routing/network/path lines, glowing connection trails, translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, progress bars, badges, labels, fake app interfaces, neon, generic AI-looking tech illustration, repeated beige device diagram, showroom-perfect product grid
